description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a teal blue fuselage and wings, featuring roundels on the upper and lower wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The airplane has two sets of straight, parallel wings connected by struts and wires, with a yellow nose and a wooden propeller at the front. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all accented with yellow and red striping. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The cockpit is open and positioned just behind the upper wing, likely designed for a single pilot. The overall design is lightweight, with visible structural elements and a streamlined, narrow fuselage.
description: A teal blue vintage biplane with dual straight wings, yellow nose, wooden propeller, and open single-seat cockpit. Features roundels on the wings and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual wings, roundels, yellow nose, and wooden propeller.
description_view_1: Angled side-top view highlighting the upper wing roundels, open cockpit, and tail markings.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, roundels, and streamlined fuselage.
description_view_3: Angled side-top view from the opposite side, showing the tail design and cockpit placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings, no winglets, and minimal dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing.
